mnemonics.title: Process States - NEW READY RUNNING WAITING TERMINATED
mnemonics.content: NEWBIE RWT - NEW BIRTH REQUIRES WAITING TILL ENDED: NEW (New), BIRTH (Ready), REQUIRES (Running), WAITING (Waiting), TILL (Terminated)

mnemonics.title: PCB Contents
mnemonics.content: SISTER GIVE ME I O - State, ID, Scheduling Info, Execution History, General Registers, Memory Info, I/O Status, Open Files

mnemonics.title: Context Switching Steps
mnemonics.content: SAVE RESTORE - Save current process state, Update PCB, Move to ready queue, Select next process, Restore next process state, Begin execution

acronyms.usage: Data structure storing all process information
Devices and operations for data transfer

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle with 5 states: NEW -> READY -> RUNNING -> WAITING -> READY, plus RUNNING -> TERMINATED and WAITING -> READY. Use arrows to show transitions between states.
Imagine a folder with sections: ID card (process info), clipboard (registers), map (memory), notebook (accounting), status report (I/O info)
Stack at top (grows down), Heap below (grows up), Data section (variables), Text section (code). Stack and Heap meet in middle.

analogies.analogy: A process is like a factory: it has an identity (PCB), resources (memory, files), and work to do (program code). The OS is like a city planner managing multiple factories.
Context switching is like reading multiple books: you bookmark your place in one book, close it, open another book, and continue reading from where you left off.
A zombie process is like unclaimed luggage at an airport: the person (child process) has left (terminated), but their belongings (exit status) are still waiting to be collected by someone (parent).
